sections.paragraph:
- Original: κεφαλή

- Transliteration: Kephale

- Phonetic: kef-al-ay'

- Definition:

1. the head, both of men and often of animals. Since the loss of the head destroys life, this word is used in the phrases relating to capital and extreme punishment.

2. metaph. anything supreme, chief, prominent

a. of persons, master lord: of a husband in relation to his wife

b. of Christ: the Lord of the husband and of the Church

c. of things: the corner stone

- Origin: from the primary kapto (in the sense of seizing)

- TDNT entry: 14:13,4

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Feminine

- Strong's: Probably from the primary word κάπτω kaptō (in the sense of seizing); the head (as the part most readily taken hold of) literally or figuratively: - head.
